professional_summary:
Highly motivated problem solver, effective communicator, and technician seeking transition from the U.S. Navy into a civilian career.
Successfully completed 1 overseas deployment. Works well with team members and groups from diverse backgrounds. Experienced in technical
troubleshooting, team management, administrative accountability, and supply management.

experience.description:
© Created new accounts, reset passwords and configured access to servers and file management software for users.
¢ Built and maintained successful relationships with service providers, vendors, dealers and consumers.

¢ Maintained records, logs and lifecycle documentation of work requests.

Reviewed requests for service to resolve IT difficulties, documenting and fielding tickets to improve problem resolution.

Monitored IT use to maintain compliance with established processes, policies and guidelines.

Tracked and prioritized IT tickets and requests based on severity and potential business disruptions involved.
e Maintained working knowledge of current hardware and equipment by completing required training and reading applicable literature.
¢ Maintained strong call control and quickly worked through scripts to address problems.

Delivered fast, friendly and knowledgeable service for routine questions and service complaints.
Helped clients navigate online systems within established frameworks to obtain services.

© Inspected, replaced and repaired defective parts and completed preventive maintenance.

Created and assigned work orders, delegated tasks and supervised work.
Oversaw technicians and delegated duties.

Monitored work order completion and worked with other team members on complex projects.
Maneuvered forklift over and around loaded pallets, boxes and other materials.
e Maintained current forklift training and certification as required by company policies.

¢ Performed troubleshooting and diagnostic procedures on systems and components of forklift models.
Read and followed schematics and diagrams for mechanical, electrical and hydraulic systems.
Assessed equipment to determine cause of issue or malfunction and identified appropriate repairs.

Explained technology-related details in easy-to-understand terms to individuals from different backgrounds and in various job positions.

e Graduated Boot camp April of 2014
© Promoted to the rank of E-2
-Lead radar technician
-Operated and performed maintenance on all missile launch functions
-Aided senior leadership by generating reports for executive decision-making support
-Directed and controlled crowds at base functions and organized and recorded facts to prepare incident reports
-Performed fault isolation radar systems for deployment readiness
- Volunteered for the United States Honor Guard as team lead
- I've led over 25 ceremonies for naturalizations resulting in 315 new US citizens
© Mentored over 3k sailor freshly out of boot camp with personnel and clerical duties
e 2015 promoted to the rank of E-3
e Barracks Supervisor
-Led room inspections for cleanliness and uniform inspects
© 2015-17 stationed in Virginia Beach for training
© 2016 promoted to 3rd class petty officer
© 2017 stationed in San Diego C._A

¢ Supplied accurate course and technique information.
© Maintained detailed records of what customers wanted and ordered, helping to increase profits when those customers returned at later date.
© Provided onsite training.
© Input customer data using Microsoft software and made immediate updates to reflect room changes.
© Provided excellent and professional service to ensure guest satisfaction.
© Processed incoming mail and packages and placed envelopes in bins to be dispersed to personnel in different staffing departments.
© Closed down kitchen after each shift and precisely followed closing checklist for kitchen stations.
